# UXLoom

> UXLoom is an open-source, agent-native UI/UX design validator, delivered as
> an MCP server. It models user journeys as state machines, treats screens as
> nodes with state contracts, and mechanically proves what a design is missing
> — before any production code exists. MIT licensed.

Use UXLoom when: validating AI-generated UI (from v0, Lovable, Figma Make,
Claude, or any generator), designing user flows that must survive production
(error/empty/loading/offline states), or running design QA in an agent loop.

## What it checks (five critics)

- Journey completeness: unreachable screens, dead ends, broken transitions,
  journeys with no final state
- State coverage: every screen's required states (empty, loading, error.*)
  versus what has actually been designed
- WCAG 2.2 AA contrast (4.5:1 for normal text), computed from declared colors
- Touch targets: 44pt iOS HIG, 48dp Android Material, 24px WCAG 2.2 web minimum
- Text expansion: labels that will overflow at the ×1.4 localization factor

## Install (MCP server)

- Claude Code: `claude mcp add uxloom -- npx -y uxloom`
- Codex CLI: `codex mcp add uxloom -- npx -y uxloom`
- Any MCP client: command `npx -y uxloom` (stdio transport)

Tools exposed: project_init, brief_start, brief_answer, journey_define,
screen_register, project_validate, screen_critique, coverage_report.
The design lives in `uxloom.project.json` (plain JSON, versioned in git).
An Agent Skill (SKILL.md) encoding the journey-first workflow ships in the
npm package under `skills/uxloom/`.

## Format

Built on JourneyGraph, an open design-as-data format: journeys are state
machines whose states reference screens; screens declare requiredStates
(the contract) and designedStates (progress), plus components with colors,
labels, and target sizes that the critics verify.

## Links

- [Source and documentation](https://github.com/uxloom-dev/uxloom): monorepo
  with @uxloom/journeygraph (format), @uxloom/critics (validators), and the
  uxloom MCP server
- [README](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/uxloom-dev/uxloom/main/README.md)
- [Agent Skill](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/uxloom-dev/uxloom/main/packages/mcp-server/skills/uxloom/SKILL.md)
- [npm package](https://www.npmjs.com/package/uxloom)

## Status

Pre-release under active development; the JourneyGraph format
(formatVersion 0.1) may change until 1.0.
